hook.text: You think you're talking to your bank. An attacker is secretly sitting between you, reading every message.

buildup.text: The attacker intercepts traffic between two parties. Both sides think they're talking directly to each other.

discovery.text: On public Wi-Fi, ARP spoofing lets an attacker redirect your traffic through their device. You'd never notice.

twist.text: HTTPS stops MITM for web traffic. Attackers can't fake the certificate without triggering browser warnings.

climax.text: But if you click 'proceed anyway' on a certificate warning, you're handing your data to the attacker voluntarily.

punchline.text: MITM attacks hijack the conversation. HTTPS is the antidote.

quiz.question: How does HTTPS prevent man-in-the-middle attacks?
options: The server's certificate proves its identity and encrypts traffic
It blocks all traffic on public Wi-Fi
It scans incoming packets for malware
